message.content:
{
  "lecture_id": "linear_algebra_001",
  "knowledge_objects": [
    {
      "id": "vector_space",
      "name": "Vector Space",
      "type": "Concept",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "A set of objects called vectors that can be added together and multiplied by scalars while satisfying the vector space axioms.",
      "source_span": "A vector space is a set of objects called vectors that can be added together and multiplied by scalars while satisfying the vector space axioms."
    },
    {
      "id": "basis",
      "name": "Basis",
      "type": "Concept",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "A set of independent vectors that can represent every vector in the space through linear combinations.",
      "source_span": "A basis is a set of independent vectors that can represent every vector in the space through linear combinations."
    },
    {
      "id": "dimension",
      "name": "Dimension",
      "type": "Concept",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "The number of vectors in a basis of a vector space.",
      "source_span": "The number of vectors in a basis is the dimension of the vector space."
    },
    {
      "id": "matrix",
      "name": "Matrix",
      "type": "Concept",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "A rectangular array of numbers that can represent a linear transformation once bases have been chosen for the input and output spaces.",
      "source_span": "A matrix is a rectangular array of numbers that can represent a linear transformation once bases have been chosen for the input and output spaces."
    },
    {
      "id": "linear_transformation",
      "name": "Linear Transformation",
      "type": "Concept",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "A mapping between vector spaces that can be represented by a matrix once bases are chosen.",
      "source_span": "a linear transformation"
    },
    {
      "id": "matrix_multiplication",
      "name": "Matrix Multiplication",
      "type": "Method",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "The operation that corresponds to composing linear transformations.",
      "source_span": "Matrix multiplication corresponds to composing linear transformations"
    },
    {
      "id": "eigenvector",
      "name": "Eigenvector",
      "type": "Concept",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "A nonzero vector whose direction is preserved by the transformation represented by a square matrix.",
      "source_span": "an eigenvector is a nonzero vector v whose direction is preserved by the transformation represented by A"
    },
    {
      "id": "eigenvalue",
      "name": "Eigenvalue",
      "type": "Concept",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "The scalar factor by which an eigenvector is scaled under a linear transformation.",
      "source_span": "The corresponding eigenvalue λ satisfies Av = λ v."
    },
    {
      "id": "characteristic_polynomial",
      "name": "Characteristic Polynomial",
      "type": "Concept",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "The polynomial det(λI - A) whose roots are the eigenvalues of a square matrix.",
      "source_span": "the characteristic polynomial det(λI - A)"
    },
    {
      "id": "eigenvalue_equation",
      "name": "Eigenvalue Equation",
      "type": "Formula",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "The equation Av = λ v relating a matrix, its eigenvector, and eigenvalue.",
      "source_span": "Av = λ v"
    }
  ]
}
